Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 14:02 ET
Dispersion trade signals flash as index hedging surges while single-name call buying accelerates
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index P/C at 1.60 versus single-name P/C at 0.72 signals institutional dispersion positioning ahead of earnings season.
  • 2. Implied correlation at 0.08 confirms stock-specific regime with multiple vol divergence flags across major ETFs.
  • 3. GOOG massive block flow targeting April expiry suggests informed positioning ahead of potential AI developments.
NARRATIVE — dispersion opportunity

The most compelling signal today is the extreme P/C divergence between indices (1.60) and single names (0.72) — a 0.88 spread that ranks in the 95th percentile historically. This pattern typically reflects institutional dispersion hedges, where managers buy index puts as portfolio insurance while maintaining bullish single-name exposure through targeted call purchases. The 0.08 implied correlation reading confirms this stock-specific environment, creating a textbook setup for long single-name vol, short index vol trades. GOOG dominates unusual flow with two massive April call blocks totaling 11,588 contracts across the 302.5C and 310C strikes, representing $121M in premium. The April 17 expiry timing suggests positioning around potential AI product announcements or earnings surprises, particularly given Google's recent AI infrastructure investments. This flow stands out against the broader tech divergence flags, where QQQ shows cheap vol/rich skew while individual tech names like AAPL exhibit vol high/skew flat patterns. Multiple vol surface anomalies reinforce the dispersion theme, with six major ETFs (IBIT, XBI, KWEB, XLK, IYT, QQQ) flagging cheap vol/rich skew divergences. The positive gamma environment ($5.9B SPY GEX) should dampen index moves while allowing single-name volatility to express itself — exactly the conditions dispersion traders seek heading into next week's earnings from GS, JPM, and NFLX.

Layer 1: Sentiment +15Layer 2: GEX +$5.9BLayer 3: P/C divergence 0.88Layer 4: IC 0.08

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
